old_claim.note: Atomic Clocks provides a capability that enables this technology without being the only possible path.

new_claim.note: GPS determines range from precise signal travel time, and its satellites therefore require synchronized atomic clocks to provide usable positioning and timing.

source_shape.source_locator: Opening section: states that positioning satellites are powered by atomic clocks and that GPS would not function without their precise timing.
source_shape.source_claim_summary: NIST directly identifies precise atomic time as the signal-timing basis that allows GPS receivers to calculate position.
source_shape.source_support_rationale: Ranging error grows directly from clock error, so ordinary unsynchronized clocks would destroy the measurement needed by the scoped positioning system.

ontology_before: Atomic clocks were modeled as an optional enhancement to GPS.
ontology_after: Synchronized atomic clocks are a required timing mechanism for GPS ranging and time distribution.
invariant_preserved_or_changed: Changed and locked by graph invariant 2026-07-16-gps-navstar-logistics-boundary: atomic_clocks remains required for gps.
would_reject_if: A source-backed GPS architecture is demonstrated that obtains equivalent global ranging without precise synchronized clocks.
short_reason: NIST states that GPS would not function without the precise timing supplied by atomic clocks.
